prompts.abstract: A cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
prompts.description: A cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
prompts.beginner: Design a cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color. The cube measures 0.75 units in height, width, and depth.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the global axes. Draw a square on the X-Y plane, ensuring that the sides are equal in length. Scale the square to reduce its size uniformly. Transform the scaled square into a 3D object by extruding it along the Z-axis to create a cube. The cube has a height, width, and depth of 0.75 units each. The final model is a cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. On the X-Y plane, draw a single face, the first face, and within this face, create a closed loop, the first loop. Draw four lines to form a square: the first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0); the second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.75); the third line starts at (0.75, 0.75) and ends at (0.0, 0.75); the fourth line starts at (0.0, 0.75) and returns to the origin (0.0, 0.0). Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire 2D sketch. Rotate the scaled 2D sketch using the Euler angles (0.0, 0.0, 0.0) and translate it using the translation vector (0.0, 0.0, 0.0). Extrude the 2D sketch along the normal direction by 0.75 units to generate the 3D model, creating a new solid body. The final dimensions of the cube are 0.75 units in length, width, and height. The cube has smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. On the X-Y plane, draw a single face, the first face, and within this face, create a closed loop, the first loop. Draw four lines to form a square: the first line starts at the origin (0.0, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.0); the second line starts at (0.75, 0.0) and ends at (0.75, 0.75); the third line starts at (0.75, 0.75) and ends at (0.0, 0.75); the fourth line starts at (0.0, 0.75) and returns to the origin (0.0, 0.0). Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the entire 2D sketch. Rotate the scaled 2D sketch using the Euler angles (0.0, 0.0, 0.0) and translate it using the translation vector (0.0, 0.0, 0.0). Extrude the 2D sketch along the normal direction by 0.75 units to generate the 3D model, creating a new solid body. The final dimensions of the cube are 0.75 units in length, width, and height. The cube has smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the global axes. Draw a square on the X-Y plane, ensuring that the sides are equal in length. Scale the square to reduce its size uniformly. Transform the scaled square into a 3D object by extruding it along the Z-axis to create a cube. The cube has a height, width, and depth of 0.75 units each. The final model is a cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color. The cube measures 0.75 units in height, width, and depth.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rounded Cube
#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler angles to 0.0, 0.0, 0.0.
- Set the translation vector to 0.0, 0.0, 0.0.

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Draw a single face (Face 1) on the XY plane.
- Create a closed loop (Loop 1) within this face.
- Draw four lines to form a square:
  - Line 1: Start at (0.0, 0.0), end at (0.75, 0.0).
  - Line 2: Start at (0.75, 0.0), end at (0.75, 0.75).
  - Line 3: Start at (0.75, 0.75), end at (0.0, 0.75).
  - Line 4: Start at (0.0, 0.75), end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scaling factor of 0.75 to the 2D sketch.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the 2D sketch using the Euler angles (0.0, 0.0, 0.0).
- Translate the 2D sketch using the translation vector (0.0, 0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the 2D sketch along the normal direction by 0.75 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- The operation will create a new solid body.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.75 units
- Width: 0.75 units
- Height: 0.75 units

### Description
- Name: Cube
- Shape: A cube with smooth, rounded edges and a uniform gray color.
- Length: 0.75 units
- Width: 0.75 units
- Height: 0.75 units
</part_1>
